message.content:
{
  "competency_groups": [
    {
      "code": "U1-C1",
      "title": "Electric Charges and Fields Competencies",
      "description": "Competencies related to electric charges, fields, flux, and Gauss's theorem.",
      "competencies": [
        "Apply Coulomb's law and superposition principle for forces between point charges.",
        "Analyze continuous charge distributions and calculate electric field intensity.",
        "Interpret electric field lines and determine torque on an electric dipole in a uniform field.",
        "State Gauss's theorem and apply it to find fields due to infinite wire, plane sheet, and spherical shell."
      ],
      "unit_name": "Electrostatics",
      "chapter_name": "Electric Charges and Fields"
    },
    {
      "code": "U1-C2",
      "title": "Potential and Capacitance Competencies",
      "description": "Competencies related to electric potential, energy, dielectrics, and capacitors.",
      "competencies": [
        "Calculate electric potential and potential difference due to point charges, dipoles, and systems of charges.",
        "Identify equipotential surfaces and calculate electrical potential energy for charge systems.",
        "Distinguish between conductors and insulators regarding free and bound charges.",
        "Explain dielectric polarization and capacitance concepts including series/parallel combinations.",
        "Derive or recall formulae for parallel plate capacitors with and without dielectrics."
      ],
      "unit_name": "Electrostatics",
      "chapter_name": "Electrostatic Potential and Capacitance"
    },
    {
      "code": "U2-C3",
      "title": "Current Electricity Competencies",
      "description": "Competencies related to current, Ohm's law, resistivity, cells, and circuits.",
      "competencies": [
        "Relate drift velocity and mobility to electric current in metallic conductors.",
        "Analyze V-I characteristics (linear/non-linear) and calculate electrical energy/power/resistivity.",
        "Explain temperature dependence of resistance and internal resistance of a cell.",
        "Apply Kirchhoff's rules and Wheatstone bridge principles for circuit analysis."
      ],
      "unit_name": "Current Electricity",
      "chapter_name": "Current Electricity"
    },
    {
      "code": "U3-C4",
      "title": "Moving Charges Competencies",
      "description": "Competencies related to magnetic fields, Biot-Savart law, Ampere's law, and forces.",
      "competencies": [
        "Describe Oersted's experiment and apply Biot-Savart law for circular loops.",
        "Apply Ampere's law for infinite straight wires and solenoids (qualitative).",
        "Calculate force on moving charges in uniform magnetic/electric fields.",
        "Determine torque on current loops and define ampere based on forces between conductors."
      ],
      "unit_name": "Magnetic Effects of Current and Magnetism",
      "chapter_name": "Moving Charges and Magnetism"
    },
    {
      "code": "U3-C5",
      "title": "Magnetism and Matter Competencies",
      "description": "Competencies related to magnetic dipoles, materials, and properties.",
      "competencies": [
        "Model bar magnets as equivalent solenoids (qualitative).",
        "Analyze torque on magnetic dipoles in uniform fields and interpret magnetic field lines.",
        "Classify substances based on para-, dia-, and ferro-magnetic properties with examples.",
        "Explain magnetization, temperature effects, and dipole moments."
      ],
      "unit_name": "Magnetic Effects of Current and Magnetism",
      "chapter_name": "Magnetism and Matter"
    },
    {
      "code": "U4-C6",
      "title": "Electromagnetic Induction Competencies",
      "description": "Competencies related to induction, Lenz's law, and self/mutual inductance.",
      "competencies": [
        "State Faraday's laws of electromagnetic induction regarding induced EMF and current.",
        "Apply Lenz's Law to determine direction of induced currents.",
        "Explain concepts of self-induction and mutual induction."
      ],
      "unit_name": "Electromagnetic Induction and Alternating Currents",
      "chapter_name": "Electromagnetic Induction"
    },
    {
      "code": "U4-C7",
      "title": "Alternating Current Competencies",
      "description": "Competencies related to AC circuits, resonance, power, and transformers.",
      "competencies": [
        "Distinguish between peak and RMS values of current/voltage in AC circuits.",
        "Analyze reactance, impedance, and LCR series circuit phasors (qualitative).",
        "Explain resonance phenomena and calculate power factor/wattless current.",
        "Describe the working principles of AC generators and transformers."
      ],
      "unit_name": "Electromagnetic Induction and Alternating Currents",
      "chapter_name": "Alternating Current"
    },
    {
      "code": "U5-C8",
      "title": "Electromagnetic Waves Competencies",
      "description": "Competencies related to wave characteristics, spectrum, and displacement current.",
      "competencies": [
        "Explain the concept of displacement current and generation of electromagnetic waves.",
        "Describe transverse nature (qualitative) and characteristics of EM waves.",
        "Identify regions of the electromagnetic spectrum and their elementary uses."
      ],
      "unit_name": "Electromagnetic Waves",
      "chapter_name": "Electromagnetic Waves"
    },
    {
      "code": "U6-C9",
      "title": "Ray Optics Competencies",
      "description": "Competencies related to reflection, refraction, lenses, and optical instruments.",
      "competencies": [
        "Apply mirror formulae and lens maker's formula for spherical mirrors and thin lenses.",
        "Analyze total internal reflection and its application in optical fibers.",
        "Calculate magnification and power of lenses; analyze combinations of thin lenses.",
        "Explain refraction through prisms and working principles of microscopes/telescopes."
      ],
      "unit_name": "Optics",
      "chapter_name": "Ray Optics and Optical Instruments"
    },
    {
      "code": "U6-C10",
      "title": "Wave Optics Competencies",
      "description": "Competencies related to wave fronts, interference, diffraction, and Huygen's principle.",
      "competencies": [
        "Apply Huygen's principle to prove laws of reflection and refraction for plane waves.",
        "Analyze conditions for sustained interference and calculate fringe width in Young's experiment (final expression only).",
        "Explain single-slit diffraction qualitatively, including central maxima width."
      ],
      "unit_name": "Optics",
      "chapter_name": "Wave Optics"
    },
    {
      "code": "U7-C11",
      "title": "Dual Nature Competencies",
      "description": "Competencies related to photoelectric effect and matter waves.",
      "competencies": [
        "Explain Hertz/Lenard observations and Einstein's photoelectric equation (particle nature).",
        "Interpret experimental study of the photoelectric effect.",
        "Apply de-Broglie relation for wave-particle duality of matter."
      ],
      "unit_name": "Dual Nature of Radiation and Matter",
      "chapter_name": "Dual Nature of Radiation and Matter"
    },
    {
      "code": "U8-C12",
      "title": "Atoms Competencies",
      "description": "Competencies related to atomic models, spectra, and Bohr's theory.",
      "competencies": [
        "Describe Rutherford's alpha-particle scattering experiment and model of the atom.",
        "Apply Bohr's postulates for hydrogen: radius, velocity, energy expressions for nth orbit.",
        "Explain hydrogen line spectra qualitatively."
      ],
      "unit_name": "Atoms and Nuclei",
      "chapter_name": "Atoms"
    },
    {
      "code": "U8-C13",
      "title": "Nuclei Competencies",
      "description": "Competencies related to nuclear structure, forces, fission, and fusion.",
      "competencies": [
        "Describe composition/size of nucleus and nature of nuclear force.",
        "Apply mass-energy relation (E=mc²) and concepts of mass defect/binding energy per nucleon.",
        "Explain variations in binding energy with mass number.",
        "Differentiate between nuclear fission and fusion processes."
      ],
      "unit_name": "Atoms and Nuclei",
      "chapter_name": "Nuclei"
    },
    {
      "code": "U9-C14",
      "title": "Semiconductor Electronics Competencies",
      "description": "Competencies related to energy bands, semiconductors, diodes, and rectifiers.",
      "competencies": [
        "Distinguish conductors, insulators, and semiconductors based on energy band theory (qualitative).",
        "Differentiate between intrinsic/extrinsic (p-type/n-type) semiconductors.",
        "Analyze p-n junction formation and I-V characteristics in forward/reverse bias.",
        "Explain the application of diodes as rectifiers."
      ],
      "unit_name": "Electronic Devices",
      "chapter_name": "Semiconductor Electronics: Materials, Devices and Simple Circuits"
    }
  ]
}
